LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS LENAHAN LEE SLATER & PEARSE LLP, WILLIAM MAJERNIK, JR AND CHARLETON PEARSE'S MOTION TO QUASH PURPORTED SERVICE OF SUMMONS. NOTICE WAS NOT GIVEN TO THE OPPOSING PARTY AFTER THE TENTATIVE RULING CAME OUT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - THE MOTION IS DENIED AS MOOT. THE SERVICE OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS MOOT BECAUSE PLAINTIFF FILED A SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT THAT SUPERSEDES THAT PLEADING. THE COURT CANNOT DETERMINE WHETHER PLAINTIFF PROPERLY SERVED THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

MAJERNIK, JR., AND CHARLETON S. PEARCE. NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE PROOFS OF SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ON THESE DEFENDANTS ARE DEFECTIVE. FIRST, THE PROCESS SERVER HAS NOT DESIGNATED A METHOD OF SERVICE. (AMBROZY DECL., EXHIBITS A - C.) SECOND, TO THE EXTENT THAT MR. CATO MAY BE ASSERTING SERVICE BY MAIL AND ACKNOWLEDGEMENT OF RECEIPT OF SERVICE, THE PROCESS SERVER HAS NOT ATTACHED A COMPLETED NOTICE AND ACKNOWLEDGEMENT OF RECEIPT FOR EACH DEFENDANT PER CCP 415.30. Notice Of Motion To Quash Purported Service Of Summons
Off Calendar Apr-05-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MARK CATO, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER.
CHARLETON S. PEARSE, ESQ., 916.443.1030, FOR DEFENDANTS LENAHAN LEE SLATER & PEARSE LLP, CHARLETON S. PEARSE AND WILLIAM R. MAJERNIK JR.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTs LENAHAN LEE SLATER & PEARSE LLP, WILLIAM MAJERNIK, JR & CHARLETON PEARSE's Motion To Quash Purported Service Of Summons is granted as to defendants Lenahan, Lee, Slater & Pearse, LLP, William R. Majernik, Jr., and Charleton S. Pearce. No opposition filed. The proofs of service of Summons and the Second Amended Complaint on these defendants are defective. First, the process server has not designated a method of service. (Ambrozy Decl., Exhibits A - C.) Second, to the extent that Mr. Cato may be asserting service by mail and acknowledgement of receipt of service, the process server has not attached a completed Notice and Acknowledgement of Receipt for each defendant per CCP 415.30. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
